conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with the first question: **Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me how many children you have and their ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 16 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have a few questions to help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Is Alex currently in full-time, non-advanced education or approved training?** For example, is he attending school, college, or an approved training program?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's still in college doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship with Alex.

**Are you Alex's parent, or do you have parental responsibility for him? Or are you perhaps looking after Alex in some other capacity** (for example, as a foster carer or other guardian)?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask about your residency status.

**Do you normally live in the UK?** And if yes, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status, or another immigration status?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Could you please also confirm **your immigration status in the UK?** For example, are you:

- A UK citizen
- An EU/EEA citizen with settled or pre-settled status
- Someone with indefinite leave to remain
- Or another status?

This helps me determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

**Does Alex normally live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** For example, Alex's other parent or another person?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ You are Alex's parent with parental responsibility
✓ You are a UK citizen living in the UK
✓ Alex lives with you
✓ Alex is 16 and in approved full-time non-advanced education (A-levels)
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

Since Alex is under 20 and still in approved full-time education (A-levels at college), you meet all the requirements to claim Child Benefit for him.

**Important note:** Alex can continue to be included in your Child Benefit claim until he turns 20, as long as he remains in approved full-time non-advanced education or training. If his education status changes (for example, if he leaves college or finishes his A-levels and doesn't continue in approved education), you'll need to notify the Child Benefit Office.

You can apply for Child Benefit if you haven't already done so. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old and is in full-time non-advanced education (doing A-levels at college). Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim for children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training. The claimant is Alex's parent, lives in the UK (Manchester), is a UK citizen, and Alex lives with them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

The claimant meets all the key requirements: they are Alex's parent with parental responsibility, they are a UK citizen living in the UK, Alex lives with them, Alex is under 20 and in approved full-time non-advanced education (A-levels), and no one else is claiming for Alex.
